HeroContent.title: HeroContent
HeroContent.description: Represents the dynamic content for the hero section of the landing page, including the main headline, subheadline, and call-to-action.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HeroContent entity.

headline.description: The main compelling headline for the hero section, highlighting the value proposition.

subheadline.description: The supporting subheadline that further articulates the cloud provider's value proposition for enterprise clients.

callToActionText.description: Text displayed on the call-to-action button in the hero section, e.g., 'Request a Consultation'.

callToActionUrl.description: The URL link for the call-to-action button in the hero section.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the hero content was last updated, often by the AI Content Assistant Tool.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Details of a top cloud product offered by ApertureCloud, including its name, description, pricing, and key features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the cloud product featured on the landing page.

description.description: A clear description of the product's functionalities and benefits.

priceTierDescription.description: A descriptive string for the pricing tier, designed to be transparent (e.g., 'Starting from $X/month', 'Custom Pricing Available').

features.description: A list of prominent features or benefits associated with this product.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image or icon that visually represents the product.

displayOrder.description: A numerical value indicating the order in which this product should be displayed in the showcase section.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Represents an authentic customer testimonial or success story, including the quote, customer details, and company information with logo.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

quote.description: The direct quote from the customer testimonial.

customerName.description: The name of the customer who provided the testimonial.

customerTitle.description: The job title or role of the customer (e.g., 'CTO', 'Head of IT').

companyName.description: The name of the company the customer represents.

companyLogoUrl.description: URL to the logo of the company associated with the testimonial, used to build trust.

displayOrder.description: A numerical value indicating the order in which this testimonial should be displayed in the client success stories section.

ConsultationRequest.title: ConsultationRequest
ConsultationRequest.description: Represents a request submitted by an enterprise business to 'Request a Consultation' with ApertureCloud.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConsultationRequest entity.

companyName.description: The name of the enterprise business requesting the consultation.

contactPersonName.description: The full name of the primary contact person from the requesting company.

contactPersonEmail.description: The email address of the primary contact person for communication regarding the consultation.

contactPersonPhone.description: The phone number of the primary contact person. (Optional)

message.description: An optional message or specific query provided by the requester, detailing their needs or interests.

requestDate.description: The timestamp when the consultation request was submitted.

status.description: The current processing status of the consultation request (e.g., 'New', 'Pending', 'Contacted', 'Archived').

definition.description: Stores dynamic content for the landing page's hero section. Publicly readable for all users, write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: heroContentId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific hero content configuration.

definition.description: Contains details of top cloud products featured on the landing page. Publicly readable for all users, write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: Stores authentic customer testimonials and success stories. Publicly readable for all users, write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific testimonial.

definition.description: Records enterprise consultation requests submitted via the landing page form. Allows anonymous creation; read access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: requestId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific consultation request.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). Stores UIDs of users who have administrative privileges. The mere existence of a document here for a given UID signifies admin status. This conceptual 'AdminRole' entity is used for authorization purposes, and its schema is implicitly a marker (e.g., { 'grantedAt': timestamp }). This collection is typically managed manually by secure backend processes, not directly by client-side rules.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The UID of the administrative user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the ApertureCloud landing page's content, consultation requests, and administrative access with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to the core design principles.

**Authorization Independence:** This design primarily achieves authorization independence by using top-level collections for content (`hero_content`, `products`, `testimonials`) that are either publicly readable or restrict write access based on a top-level admin role check using `exists()`. For `ConsultationRequest` documents, anonymous users can create new requests, and administrators can read them, with no hierarchical dependencies. This avoids any `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. The `exists()` check for admin roles is performed directly on the `app_roles/admin_users/{adminUserId}` path, which is a peer-level check, not a hierarchical dependency on parent document data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   For `hero_content`, `products`, and `testimonials` collections, all documents within them share the same security posture: public read access. This means clients can perform `list` queries on these collections without rules acting as filters, as all documents are accessible. This perfectly supports QAPs.
*   For `consultation_requests`, anonymous users can `create` documents, but have no `read` access. Only authenticated administrators (whose UIDs are present in `app_roles/admin_users`) have `read` access. This allows administrators to perform `list` operations on `consultation_requests` efficiently, as all documents in this collection are readable by them. This also supports QAPs by ensuring that `list` operations for admins return all matching documents without further client-side filtering being necessary for authorization.

**Structural Segregation:** Each major entity type (`HeroContent`, `Product`, `Testimonial`, `ConsultationRequest`, `AdminRole`) is placed in its own top-level collection. This ensures that all documents within a given collection share a homogeneous security posture. For example, all `Product` documents are publicly readable, and all `ConsultationRequest` documents are anonymously writable but admin-readable. This greatly simplifies security rules by removing the need for complex conditional logic based on document content within a single collection.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Public Data:** `hero_content`, `products`, `testimonials` are structured as simple top-level collections, allowing public read access for the landing page.
*   **Anonymous Write/Admin Read Data:** `consultation_requests` is a top-level collection allowing unauthenticated users to create documents and administrators to read them.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** A dedicated collection `/app_roles/admin_users/{adminUserId}` is used to store the UIDs of administrative users. The mere existence of a document at `/app_roles/admin_users/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges. This design avoids custom claims, relies solely on `request.auth.uid`, and makes administrative authorization explicit and debuggable.
